name: White-Wheat Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk, then stir in the remaining ingredients (salt and margarine). Cool to lukewarm.
2. Measure warm water between 110-115°F. Add yeast and sugar to a large bowl; stir until dissolved. Add the milk mixture and 4 cups of white bread flour and 2 cups whole-grain wheat flour. Beat until smooth (mix on medium speed for 5 minutes using regular mixer beaters).
3. Gradually add 1 cup whole-grain wheat flour and 3 cups white flour to make a stiff dough. Knead with dough hooks until smooth and free from the sides and bottom of the bowl.
4. Transfer dough to a greased large warm bowl (add 1 tablespoon oil). Turn to grease the top, cover, and let rise in a warm place (80-85°F) until doubled in bulk (about 70 minutes).
5. Punch down, cover, let rest for 15 minutes.
6. Divide into 4 pieces; roll each piece into a rectangle measuring 14 x 9 inches. Shape into loaves and place in greased baking pans (9 x 5 x 3 inches). Cover, let rise until doubled (about 1 hour).
7. Bake at 375-400°F for 30 minutes.
8. Test for double by pressing down with two fingers 1/2 inch into the dough; if the indentation stays, it's ready.
Do not overbake. It is better to have the oven set between 350 and 400°F than 400-450°F. Most ovens run a little hot; use an oven temperature gauge if unsure of your oven's temperature.
